allowed_scope: Use Claude Code and Codex-era materials to show repository-work packaging, context management, tool-connected loops, and workflow categories with source attribution.
prohibited_scope: Do not infer productivity lift, adoption, revenue, developer replacement, autonomous engineering, current UI, or enterprise outcome.
before_promotion_gate: Any final visual needs either source-card layout with quote review or browser screenshot with raster hash and rights review.

allowed_scope: Use Claude Code security material to show permission/review boundaries and action-control framing.
prohibited_scope: Do not infer secure-by-default agents, solved safety, incident prevention, compliance, or reliable real-world control.
before_promotion_gate: Final caption must say boundary/source framing, not outcome proof.

allowed_scope: Use SWE-bench and LiveCodeBench to explain repository repair and contamination-aware coding evaluation as benchmark families.
prohibited_scope: Do not infer best coding model, autonomous engineer, productivity, secure code quality, production readiness, or broad software-engineering ability.
before_promotion_gate: No numeric score or rank may appear until benchmark fields and dated result source are normalized.

allowed_scope: Use vendor launch pages and benchmark papers together only to explain why coding-agent claims need scaffold/date fields.
prohibited_scope: Do not launder vendor benchmark claims into neutral leaderboards or independent productivity evidence.
before_promotion_gate: Any Claude 4 or Claude Code benchmark caption must cite exact row extraction and keep C-0013 active until normalized.
